name: Cloisonné Enamel

visual_attributes.morphology: metal objects with intricate decorative surfaces
distinctive_features: compartments separated by metal strips or wires
use of vitreous enamel, gemstones, or glass inlays
visible metal strips in finished pieces
visual_attributes.texture: smooth and glossy with raised metal outlines
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies from small jewelry pieces to large vessels
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: compartments arranged in geometric or pictorial designs

domain_specific_functional.CULTURAL_ROLE: used in religious and ceremonial objects
domain_specific_functional.HISTORICAL_USE: jewelry, small fittings, and large vessels

relationships.description: Byzantine techniques influenced the development of cloisonné enamel.
Cloisonné enamel was widely used in Chinese art for large vessels.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents artistic and technical achievement in metalwork

contrastive_constraints: NOT plain metal objects without compartments
NOT limited to a single cultural context
